items.title: London artist Georgia Semple explores tension between perception and reality
items.description: Her paintings are rich, layered works that feature distorted or warped figures – something that Georgia Semple says represents the "loss of perception" that one experiences throughout one's life. Speaking to FRANCE 24, the London-based artist explains how themes of faith and addiction as well as her Guyanese heritage are expressed in her thought-provoking works. She says she hopes her paintings might prompt viewers to question "their own truths" or "how faith might show up in their lives".

items.title: French hospitals 'better prepared': 'Important lessons learned from deadly 2003 heatwave'
items.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Antoine Flahault, Epidemiologist, Researcher, and Professor at Université Paris Cité - Hospital Bichat. France's latest heatwave has once again exposed healthcare systems were largely designed for another era. While the immediate focus often falls on soaring temperatures and overcrowded emergency departments, Dr. Flahault argues that the deeper issue is institutional adaptation. He suggests that France has learned important lessons since the deadly 2003 heatwave, with hospitals adopting better clinical practices and emergency planning. Yet as heatwaves become more frequent and intense, protecting vulnerable populations will increasingly require structural investment, particularly widespread access to air conditioning in hospitals, care homes, schools and, for those most at risk, private homes.

items.title: Jazz à Vienne celebrates late Miles Davis for 45th edition
items.description: In this edition of arts24, Marjorie Hache is in the town of Vienne in southeastern France, where the 45th edition of "Jazz à Vienne" is taking place. Headliners include the likes of Jon Batiste, Marcus Miller, Buena Vista Social Club and Jeff Mills. The festival is also shining a spotlight on late jazz legend Miles Davis, 100 years since his birth. The American musician played at the festival four times during his life and left a lasting mark on the town. Marjorie also shares one of her festival highlights: Cameroonian band Akutuk's concert in a swimming pool in an ode to their ancestral links with water. She also chats with New Orleans trumpeter James Andrews, who is transforming the streets of Vienne into a giant parade.

items.title: 'Pre-existing vulnerabilities: Venezuela already experiencing economic turmoil, displacement crisis'
items.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Elinor Raikes. Vice President and Head of Program Delivery at the International Rescue Committee. According to Raikes, earthquakes expose and amplify the vulnerabilities that already exist beneath the surface of a society. She suggests that the devastation unfolding in Venezuela is not solely the consequence of seismic forces, but the collision of a major natural disaster exacerbated by economic collapse, displacement, weakened public services, and shrinking humanitarian funding. Rakes warns the earthquake is not a short-term emergency but rather the beginning of a prolonged humanitarian crisis. She emphasises the logistical obstacles confronting aid agencies, the importance of coordination between international and local organisations, and the reality that recovery will be measured in months rather than weeks.

items.description: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

items.title: BBC at Venezuela baseball stadium sheltering earthquake survivors
items.description: The survivors are hopeful the authorities will find them some form of temporary accommodation while the country rebuilds - for now they are dependant on donated food and clothing.

items.title: France's 2027 presidential election: Can the far-right National Rally win without Le Pen?
items.description: With less than a year to go until France's April 2027 presidential election, the far-right National Rally party still hasn't designated a candidate. As Marine Le Pen appeals a graft conviction that barred her from seeking office for five years, her young protégé Jordan Bardella is poised to step in. But some doubt whether the young Bardella has what it takes to win the Élysée Palace.

items.title: Europe heatwave made possible by climate change, scientists say
items.description: Scientists say the latest findings underscore the urgency of both reducing emissions and adapting infrastructure to withstand more frequent and intense heatwaves in the years ahead.

items.title: Argentina wary of 'very tough' Cape Verde in World Cup last 32
items.description: Reigning champions face tournament debutants Cape Verde on Friday, July 3 in Miami. Leer más

items.title: World Cup 2026: Canada make history by reaching Round of 16
items.description: Canada secured a historic place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 with a 1-0 victory over South Africa in Los Angeles. Captain Stephen Eustáquio scored the only goal of the match deep into stoppage time. The Canucks will face the winners of Monday's tie between the Netherlands and Morocco on July 4 in Houston.

items.title: PM-in-waiting Andy Burnham lays out plan to fix 'broken' British politics
items.description: Andy Burnham, the British MP tipped to replace the outgoing prime minister Sir Keir Starmer, promised "radical change" to the nation's "broken" political system as he laid out his vision to lead the country on Monday.

items.title: Putin says Russia will press on despite fuel shortages
items.description: President Vladimir Putin has said Russia will press ahead with its battlefield aim of fully capturing four Ukrainian regions, rejecting diplomatic overtures from Ukraine.

items.title: Netanyahu plans ‘broad national govt.’ after Israel vote
items.description: He also hailed the U.S.-brokered agreement with Lebanon, calling it a historic achievement that dealt a blow to arch-foes Iran and Hezbollah

items.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
items.description: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

items.title: Magdeburg Christmas market attacker sentenced to life in prison
items.description: A Magdeburg court has sentenced a 51-year-old to life over his 2024 attack on a Christmas market in the city. The trained psychiatrist drove an SUV through a barricade and into a crowd, killing six and injuring hundreds.

items.title: At least five killed in shooting in Stade in northern Germany, police say
Milei settles Argentina's US$15-million UN debt
items.description: Milei administration pays off Argentina’s outstanding US$15.6-million debt with the United Nations as campaigning for Rafael Grossi's secretary-general candidacy enters key stretch. Leer más

items.title: Germany news: New heat record at 41.7 degrees Celsius
items.description: For the third consecutive day, the DWD German Weather Service has reported a record temperature. The worst of the heat is moving east and it should start cooling soon.

items.title: Moroccan court jails 29 in major drug trafficking case
items.description: A Moroccan court handed prison sentences to 29 people, including prominent politicians and sports and business figures, in an international drug trafficking and corruption scandal that has rocked the country.

items.title: World Cup football fever helps West Bank residents forget their woes
items.description: Although the Palestinian national team narrowly missed qualifying for the competition, fans have been cheering for other nations.

items.title: Two former Moroccan politicians jailed in 'Escobar of the Sahara' drug case
items.description: Throughout the proceedings, the defence argued there was no material evidence linking the former officials to the alleged crimes, saying the prosecution relied largely on the testimony of the convicted trafficker.

items.title: Americans' pride in US history and democracy dropped steeply over past decade, poll finds
items.description: Americans’ pride in their country's history or the way its democracy works has dropped noticeably over the past decade, according to a new poll from the Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.

items.title: British Embassy scores with World Cup watch parties
items.description: UK Ambassador David Cairns hosts crowds for England-Ghana and Scotland-Brazil matches – just don’t mention the results… Leer más

items.title: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
items.description: The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

items.title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
items.description: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.

items.title: Russian missile and drone attacks kill at least eight in Ukraine
items.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy condemns the 'horrific attacks' and urges allies to boost Ukraine's air defences.

items.title: Canada edges South Africa to reach World Cup round of 16
items.description: Canada will now face either the Netherlands or Morocco in Houston, with a place in the quarterfinals at stake, while South Africa exits the tournament after a spirited campaign that showcased the team's resilience on football's biggest stage

items.title: Israeli strike hits tent in central Gaza, killing three, including a child
items.description: Israeli forces fatally shot a 15-year-old Palestinian teenager during a raid in the occupied West Bank on Monday (June 29), the Palestinian health ministry said, as violence surges in the Israeli-occupied territory.

items.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
items.description: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

items.title: Algorithms, Artificial Intelligence and Milei’s ethics
items.description: Milei has a militant and narrow world view that he’s tied to a vision that integrates AI and tech billionaires. Those same players have a global agenda that they will pursue, and Argentina will only be part of that plan for as long as it is useful for them. Leer más

items.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
items.description: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

items.title: How population decline is exposing Germany's old divides
items.description: Decades after the fall of the Berlin Wall, a population slump in the east lays bare the existing divisions

items.title: Why Oman has become a key diplomatic player in Mideast war
items.description: Iran said Monday it had held its first meeting with Oman on managing the Strait of Hormuz since it signed a preliminary deal to end the Middle East war with the United States. It comes as French President Emmanuel Macron is set to host the Sultan of Oman at the Elysée Palace on Monday, with talks also lilely to focus on Hormuz. France 244s James Andre takes a look at how Oman ended up playing a key diplomatic role in the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake: Aid ramps up as rescue window closes
items.description: Venezuela was shaken by an aftershock on Monday as rescues continued. Interim president Delcy Rodriguez said she remained hopeful about rescue operations as criticism of her government grows. DW has the latest.

items.title: Indian Army field hospital begins operations in quake-hit Venezuela
items.description: “Indian Army doctors are taking care of people,” Mr. Jaiswal said, adding that the Venezuelan Vice-Health Minister and head of the Government of the Capital District of Venezuela visited the field hospital

items.title: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
items.description: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

items.title: Ukraine: Zaporizhzhia ablaze after Russian missile and drone attack leaves at least nine hurt
items.description: Russian missile and drone strikes set an office building ablaze in central Zaporizhzhia on 26 June, injuring at least nine people and triggering a major emergency response.

items.title: Venezuela declares state of emergency after powerful twin earthquakes
items.description: Two massive earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening; It remains unknown whether the incident caused fatalities, but some buildings collapsed and residents were driven into the streets in panic. Leer más

items.title: World Cup: Morocco's Ismael Saibari set for Bayern Munich
items.description: Three nationalities, five languages and soon to be resident in a fourth country: Ismael Saibari, the star of Morocco's World Cup so far, is set to join Bayern Munich. But he has business to finish with the Atlas Lions.

items.title: United States and Iran trade fresh strikes further testing 60-day truce
items.description: Tehran said it launched strikes against US targets in Bahrain and Kuwait in retaliation for an earlier strike launched by Washington on Iran.

items.title: Togo reforms unconstitutional, rules ECOWAS court
items.description: Togo’s constitutional reform establishing a parliamentary system was unconstitutional, according to the ECOWAS Court of Justice.

items.title: Hundreds of missing Ebola patients raising concern about uncontrolled spread of virus
items.description: Almost 300 people who’ve tested positive for Ebola are unaccounted for, according to the head of the Africa Centers For Disease Control.

items.title: Pakistan says carried out strikes along Afghanistan border, 29 militants killed
items.description: The strikes against the hideouts and safe havens of terrorists came in after a paramilitary Rangers headquarters in Karachi on Saturday (June 27, 2026) was attacked by militants, Information Minister Atta Tarar said

items.title: Asian stocks mixed and oil rises as US, Iran call end to latest attacks
items.description: Equity markets fluctuated, with Hong Kong, Manila, Sydney, Taipei and Wellington up but Jakarta, Seoul, Shanghai, Singapore and Tokyo down.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Sudan says China has waived $50m loan: What’s in it for Khartoum, Beijing?
items.description: China and Sudan signed off on a waiver of $50m as Sudan’s military-led government seeks support amid Western sanctions.

items.title: U.S. says Iran talks to continue, ‘both sides’ pausing strikes
items.description: Washington and Tehran will resume talks in Qatar on June 30, according to government sources

items.title: Prosus profit jumps 84% as deal spree sets stage for European push

items.description: Former Cabinet chief steps down after months of mounting political and judicial scrutiny – here’s how we got here. Leer más

items.title: Trump's face is added to select US passports for America's 250th birthday
items.description: The commemorative special edition passports will be available on 6 July, and can only be applied for in person

items.title: Two Qld state electorates to be abolished after review
items.description: Significant changes were made to 50 of Queensland's 93 electorates by the independent Queensland Redistribution Commission (QRC).

items.title: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
items.description: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

items.title: South Africa's President Ramaphosa warns anti-immigrant groups over June 30 'deadline'
items.description: South Africa's president has warned anti-immigration groups against trying to impose the so-called June 30th deadline for illegal migrants to leave the country.

items.title: Reports of Adorni's imminent departure from government ramp up
items.description: Rumours circulating that Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni will leave the government imminently as further lurid headlines emerge in the press. Leer más

items.title: Putin vows to ensure Russia’s security amid Ukraine retaliatory strikes
items.description: “We will undoubtedly overcome all the challenges facing us today, including terrorist attacks on our territory and infrastructure facilities,” Mr. Putin added.
